text:
User: remove that "tenex-edge who is human-only" thing -- simply when runing from within an agent session, an agent shouldn't see the tenex-edge who command by default [...] Assistant: The installed binary now behaves as requested: agent help omits `who`, human help includes it, and explicit agent-session `who` succeeds. The scoped restart preserved all 40 PTY supervisors. I’m removing the merged worktree now.

Assistant: Implemented, merged, installed, and restarted safely.

- Agent help hides `who`.
- Human help shows `who`.
- Explicit agent-session `tenex-edge who` now succeeds.
- [PR #438](https://github.com/pablof7z/tenex-edge/pull/438) merged; issue #437 closed.
- CI, focused tests, formatting, and clippy passed.
- Daemon restart preserved all 40 PTY supervisors.
- Merged worktree and local branch cleaned up. [...] User: don't show PTY label -- don't show working or idle or stuff like that label -- use a little badge indicator color coded -- deterministically color workspace name too -- wherever we show stuff in the console that shows a workspace name we should always color code deterministically with the same function (consistent color per project) [...] - Each workspace name has a deterministic color derived from its canonical workspace ID.
- Channels are grouped as `workspace: #channel #channel`.
- The second line is reserved for the long title and activity.
- Workspace coloring becomes a shared console-wide function used by `who`, channel listings, statusline, debug output, and the session picker—not local picker styling.
- The daemon supplies grouped workspace/channel data because it owns canonical channel ancestry.
